current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: High pressure and low cloud cover make this fishable but technical.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

caution_notes: Bright/high-pressure conditions may make fish cover-oriented and less forgiving.
Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing is tough on the Etowah River due to very warm water and bright, high-pressure conditions. Recent stocking improves your chances when using attractor and confidence flies.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 1 morning because cooler temperatures and low wind favor more comfortable conditions for trout despite the bright sky. This period supports careful, technical presentations in shaded and deeper water.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current river conditions include a water temperature of 81.5°F, flow at 108.0 cfs, gage height of 3.77 ft, and turbidity at 7.8 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: The weather features mostly clear skies with 22% average cloud cover, pressure at 30.12 inHg, and a stable high-pressure trend. Winds are light, averaging 1.6 mph with gusts up to 9.2 mph, and there is minimal rain expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely positioned in cold-water refuges, oxygenated pockets, shade, deeper runs, and undercut banks to avoid stress from warm water and bright light. Use smaller natural flies and stocked-trout attractors with careful drifts and less aggressive presentations. Be cautious as high pressure and warm temperatures increase trout wariness and stress.
